expectations.evidence: "The principle in play is DIP — Dependency Inversion"

expectations.text: States that the domain (service) must not name PostgresClient directly
expectations.evidence: "the service ... must depend only on stable abstractions. The moment PostgresClient(...) appears there, the service imports a volatile concrete"

expectations.text: Distinguishes domain-owned interface from infrastructure implementation
expectations.evidence: Cites ReportGateway from the skill's example; repository as implementation of a domain-owned gateway interface

expectations.text: Places new PostgresClient(...) at the composition root (Spring: @Configuration / @Bean)
expectations.evidence: "A @Bean method that returns a PostgresClient ... is Spring's idiomatic composition root"

expectations.text: Mentions 'crossing the curve' or directional inversion
expectations.evidence: "crossing the curve... Source-code dependencies from the service and the repository point inward toward the stable ReportGateway interface; control flows outward at runtime ... That opposition is the inversion."
